description:
Widen `closure_shapes` from `(FnId, capture_count)` to `SpecId`. Generate one typed stub per SpecId reachable from a MakeClosure callsite. MakeClosure resolves the SpecId from `[capture_descrs ++ arg_descrs_from_arrow]` and bakes the typed stub_fp into the closure heap object.

## What

- Replace `closure_shapes: BTreeMap<(FnId, usize), ()>` with `closure_shapes: BTreeMap<SpecId, ()>` in `src/ir_codegen.rs:1247-1263`.
- At each `Prim::MakeClosure` site during the collection pass, resolve the SpecId via `spec_registry.resolve(lam_fn_id, &[capture_descrs..., arg_descrs_from_arrow...])`. Arrow Descrs come from the typer's view of the closure value at this point. Panic loudly on miss.
- Update `stub_fn_ids` keying and `stub_sigs` keying to `SpecId`.
- At MakeClosure lowering (interp/JIT/AOT), pick `stub_fp = stub_fn_ids[resolved_sid]` instead of `(fn_id, captures.len())`.

## Why

A SpecId already fully pins `(FnId, capture types, arg types, ret type, frame schema)` since the lambda's entry params are `[captures..., args...]` by construction (`src/ir_lower.rs:819`). One stub per SpecId is sufficient; no extra capture-kind disambiguation needed.

This ticket only *keys* the stubs by SpecId — the stub body still dispatches to any-key (.29.12.3 makes that typed).

## Tests (TDD)

- Two MakeClosure sites of the same lambda with different capture Descrs → two distinct SpecIds → two distinct stub fps in the produced heap closures.
- Existing closure tests stay green.

## Out of scope

- Stub body changes (next ticket: .29.12.3).
title: Typed closure stubs keyed by SpecId
